title: Add Krea2 (Flux family) - Turbo variant first, full variant later
description:
Wire the Krea2 model into Cubric Vision end-to-end. Krea2 is a Flux-family image model, currently the most-hyped open-source release (fast + quality). Ships in two variants: TURBO (smaller/faster -> likely sizeTier 'low') and NON-TURBO (bigger -> likely sizeTier 'baseline'). SCOPE OF THIS CARD COVERS BOTH; PHASE 1 = TURBO ONLY. The user already has a ComfyUI workflow in progress and owns the workflow authoring; agent assists with research + app wiring. PROCEDURE: docs/add-model-playbook.md is the single source of truth - follow its checklist verbatim (shape decision, template->runtime split, loader-path/dep-filename/on-disk 3-way match, R2 upload with --s3-no-check-bucket + --bwlimit 3M, /mpic-compute-dep-hashes, models.js ModelDef, progressStages.js live bar count, new model.type consumer sweep). NOT a version bump (adding a model/op != appVersion bump). REUSE (CORRECTED by research/style-loras.md): Krea2 is Flux-LINEAGE in arch only - its conditioning/VAE stack is QWEN. VAE = qwen_image_vae.safetensors -> reuse existing dep vae-qwen-image (already on R2, zero upload). Text encoder = qwen3vl_4b_fp8_scaled.safetensors (NEW, must upload). NOT vae-flux-ae, NOT flux clip_l/t5. NEW model.type likely needed ('krea2'?) -> sweep consumers per playbook section 6 (ratios/qualityTiers now declared on the ModelDef; enhanceRecipe must map to an existing Cubric Prompt recipe, probably 'flux'). Watch the >=20GB per-FILE hot-store gate (playbook section 4) - the non-turbo weight may cross it; if so PING USER before shipping (container disk budget). CORRECTED 2026-07-10: the capture title is decided by the workflow's TIER, not a fixed string. tier-1 (legacy bare titles) = 'Output'; tier-2 (Input_/Output_ prefixed) = 'Output_Image'. Krea2 is tier-2, so 'Output_Image' is CORRECT (and 'Output_image' lowercase also matches - the check is case-insensitive, Chroma does the same). They are NOT interchangeable; never normalize one to the other. The old playbook claim that Output_Image is 'recognized NOWHERE' was STALE (pre-MPI-182) and is now fixed. Titles are still set in ComfyUI and re-exported - never hand-edit workflow JSON.

RELEASE-CHECK DRIFT (noted 2026-07-10, MPI-248 session): `poseReference` is in js/core/operationRegistry.js + js/data/commandRegistry.js but NOT in the generated operation_registry.json snapshot, so `npm run release:check` FAILS with 'operation_registry.json missing registry entry: poseReference'. This is NOT a bug â€” the snapshot regenerates at version-bump time (a /mpi-version-bump step), which has not run. Harmless for dev/run; it only gates a RELEASE. No separate card. Whoever cuts the next version bump (after the planned extra models) MUST let the bump regenerate the snapshot so release:check goes green; do NOT hand-edit operation_registry.json.
